title: Tidewater Announces an Initiative Agreement for the Production of Low-Carbon Renewable Gasoline and Renewable Diesel from the Fluid Catalytic Cracking Co-Processing at the Prince George Refinery
content:
Tidewater Midstream and Infrastructure Ltd. has entered into a significant agreement with the Government of British Columbia to advance low-carbon fuel production at its Prince George Refinery. Under the new "FCC Initiative Agreement," the company will receive BC Low Carbon Fuel Standard (BC LCFS) credits to support the co-processing of renewable gasoline and diesel through its Fluid Catalytic Cracking (FCC) infrastructure. These credits are expected to cover approximately 50% of renewable feedstock costs between May 2026 and April 2028, facilitating production rates of up to 300 barrels per day.

This initiative complements a previously announced agreement regarding hydrotreater co-processing, bringing the refineryâ€™s total renewable capacity to 600 barrels per day. Combined, these efforts are projected to lower British Columbia's carbon emissions by over 60,000 metric tonnes annually. CEO Jeremy Baines highlighted the partnership as a key step in leading the energy transition while supporting regional energy security and economic growth.
author: Analysts Account
